text.title: Predictive Analytics for PM Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Predictive Analytics for PM methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Forecasts MUST be reported as probability ranges (e.g. 'P50 4d, P85 9d, P95 18d'); single-point estimates are rejected.
rule.rationale: deterministic point estimates are systematically optimistic and lose calibration.

rule.statement: Each forecast batch MUST include a calibration report comparing prior forecasts to actuals; calibration <0.7 triggers model review.
rule.rationale: uncalibrated forecasts are noise; they must be measured to be trusted.

rule.statement: Each prediction MUST cite the dataset window + model version used; un-anchored predictions are rejected.
rule.rationale: anchored window + model version is the only way to debug a bad forecast.

rule.statement: If Monte Carlo on throughput beats an ML model on calibration, ML is forbidden (Occam's razor); ML must demonstrate uplift over baseline.
rule.rationale: ML without uplift adds complexity, technical debt, and reduces explainability.

rule.statement: Forecasts MUST be acknowledged by a named decision owner who commits to act on the signal; reports nobody acts on are deprecated.
Bias audit (e.g. forecasts always optimistic) MUST run quarterly; uncorrected systematic bias is rejected.
rule.rationale: biased forecasts lose trust faster than uncalibrated ones.
